input_candidates.truth_value: Fe-26Al-based alloys exhibit a yield stress anomaly with peak around 550°C; the peak is shifted to lower temperatures (474°C, 619 MPa) for Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C due to Laves phase.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fracture modes at room temperature (Table 2): Fe-15Al, Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C, Fe-15Al-2V-1C, Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C, Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C, Fe-26Al, Fe-26Al-2V-1C, Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C show transgranular cleavage (TGC); Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C, Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C show intergranular decohesion (IGD) plus TGC.

input_candidates.truth_value: Tension-compression asymmetry observed in all alloys, attributed to screw dislocation glide, Bauschinger effect, and notch sensitivity of matrix due to precipitates.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fe-26Al-2V-1C shows pronounced tension-compression asymmetry (~36%) due to thin plate-like VC precipitates.

input_candidates.truth_value: Room-temperature fracture modes: transgranular cleavage (TGC) for Fe-15Al-based and some Fe-26Al-based alloys; intergranular decohesion (IGD) mixed with TGC for Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C, Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Tension-compression asymmetry: All investigated alloys exhibit higher yield stress in compression than in tension at room temperature. Fe-26Al-2V-1C shows pronounced tension-compression asymmetry (~36%) attributed to thin sharp plate-like VC precipitates causing notch sensitivity. Possible contributions include nonplanar screw dislocation cores, Bauschinger effect from internal tensions during solidification, and precipitate notch sensitivity.
